page: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the arm64 architecture at 2.7 GHz, this instance features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall within the average tier, multi-core execution is highly competitive. The server achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations ranking in the top tier. However, LLM text generation benchmarks reveal a performance bottleneck, registering weak results for both small and large models, though prompt processing speeds remain strong to average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. The combination of top-tier memory bandwidth, strong database operations, and high static web serving throughput makes this server suitable for multi-threaded workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing, whereas workloads dependent on high single-core performance or local storage may require alternative configurations.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, it maintains a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between average single-core capabilities and top-tier multi-core execution, with exceptional memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded decompression. While LLM text generation speeds are relatively weak, the server excels in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. This bare-metal instance offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it highly efficient for concurrent, memory-intensive, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal arm64 server featuring 192 dedicated Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A bare-metal arm64 platform delivering 192 dedicated cores and high DDR5 memory bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical arm64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors.
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators.
Suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded processing.
